zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alexander Livingston 5th Lord Livingston of Callandar PC於1500年出生在英國時，他的父親William Livingston 4th Lord Livingston of Callendar22歲，母親Agnes Hepburn of Whitsome25歲。他於1520年之前與Janet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchan在蘇格蘭斯林史爾卡藍德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1522年登記服兵役。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Alexander Livingston 5th Lord Livingston of Callandar PC于1500年出生在英国时，他的父亲William Livingston 4th Lord Livingston of Callendar22岁，母亲Agnes Hepburn of Whitsome25岁。他于1520年之前与Janet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchan在苏格兰斯林史爾卡藍德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1522年登记服兵役。

nameMeanings.name: Livingston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a place in Lothian, originally named in Middle English as Levingston. The placename derives from the Middle English personal name Leving (genitive Levinges) + Middle English, Older Scots toun ‘town, village, settlement’.
Irish: surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Duinnshléibhe and Mac Duinnshléibhe (see Dunleavy ).

variants.name: Dunleavy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Connor, John Patrick, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Duinnshléibhe or Ó Duinnshléibhe ‘son of Donn Sléibhe’, a personal name composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + sléibhe ‘of the mountain’, meaning ‘chieftain (literally, brown one) of the mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alexander Livingston 5th Lord Livingston of Callandar PC was born in 1500, in England, his father, William Livingston 4th Lord Livingston of Callendar, was 22 and his mother, Agnes Hepburn of Whitsome, was 25. He married Janet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchan before 1520, in Callander, Stirlingshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He registered for military service in 1522.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alexander Livingston 5th Lord Livingston of Callandar PC est né en 1500, à Angleterre, son père, William Livingston 4th Lord Livingston of Callendar, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Agnes Hepburn of Whitsome, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Janet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchan avant 1520, à Callander, Stirlingshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1522.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Livingston 5th Lord Livingston of Callandar PCが1500年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William Livingston 4th Lord Livingston of Callendarは22歳，母Agnes Hepburn of Whitsomeは25歳であった。1520年以前にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアカランダーでJanet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1522年に兵役登録をした。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Livingston 5th Lord Livingston of Callandar PC(이)가 1500년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Livingston 4th Lord Livingston of Callendar(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Hepburn of Whitsome(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1520년 이전 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 캘랜더에서 Janet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1522년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Janet-nic-Alexander Stewart of Buchan перед 1520 г. в местности калландир, стирлингшир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
